title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM Reinforcement Learning System at Scale

summary.problem: Large-scale LLM reinforcement learning for reasoning suffers from entropy collapse, inefficient gradient signals, and poor training stability, with existing results lacking reproducible open-source implementations.
summary.method: DAPO introduces four techniques on top of GRPO: (1) Clip-Higher with decoupled clipping bounds to prevent entropy collapse, (2) Dynamic Sampling that filters out prompts with all-correct or all-incorrect responses, (3) Token-Level Policy Gradient Loss that normalizes across total tokens, and (4) Overlong Reward Shaping with soft graduated penalties for truncated responses.
summary.innovation: The decoupled clipping (separate upper/lower bounds) and dynamic sampling (filtering zero-gradient prompts at batch level) are novel contributions that together solve the entropy collapse and sample efficiency problems in large-scale GRPO training.
summary.results: DAPO achieves 50 points on AIME 2024 using Qwen2.5-32B, surpassing DeepSeek-R1-Zero's 47 points with 50% fewer training steps. Ablation shows each technique contributes incrementally: naive GRPO (30) -> +overlong filtering (36) -> +clip-higher (38) -> +soft overlong punishment (41) -> +token-level loss (42) -> +dynamic sampling (50).
summary.one_liner: DAPO 在 GRPO 基础上提出解耦裁剪、动态采样等四项技术，用 Qwen2.5-32B 在 AIME 2024 上达到 50 分，超过 DeepSeek-R1-Zero 且训练步数减半。

key_references.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via RL
DeepSeekMath: Pushing the Limits of Mathematical Reasoning (GRPO)
